title: Bhikkhus, for a person who transgresses one thing, I say that there is no evil deed that cannot be done. What one thing? Knowing, speaking…
content:
Bhikkhus, for a person who transgresses one thing, I say that there is no evil deed that cannot be done. What one thing? Knowing, speaking falsely.

Whoever speaks falsely,
Transgressing that one thing,
Disregarding the world beyond,
There is no evil they cannot do.

title: There are these three divine messengers, bhikkhus. What three? Here, bhikkhus, a person does evil with body, speech, and mind. After death, with the breakup…
content: There are these three divine messengers, bhikkhus. What three? Here, bhikkhus, a person does evil with body, speech, and mind. After death, with the breakup of the body, he is reborn in a plane of misery, a bad destination, a lower realm, hell. Then the hell-wardens seize him by both arms and present him to King Yama, saying: 'Your Majesty, this person did not respect his mother, did not respect his father, did not respect samaṇas, did not respect brahmins, did not honor the elders of the family. Let Your Majesty punish him!' Then King Yama questions, cross-questions, and examines him about the first divine messenger: 'Good man, did you not see the first divine messenger appearing among humans?' He replies: 'I did not see, sire.' Then, bhikkhus, King Yama says to him: 'Good man, did you never see among humans a man or a woman eighty, ninety, or a hundred years old, aged, crooked like a roof, bent over, leaning on a staff, trembling as they walk, afflicted, their youth gone, teeth fallen out, hair grey and falling, bald, wrinkled, with limbs spotted and pale?' He says: 'Yes, sire, I have seen.' Then, bhikkhus, King Yama says to him: 'Good man, being intelligent and mature, did you not think: "I too am subject to aging, I cannot escape aging. Let me do good deeds by body, speech, and mind"?' He says: 'No, sire, I did not. I was heedless, sire.' Then, bhikkhus, King Yama says to him: 'Good man, because of heedlessness, you did not do good deeds by body, speech, and mind. Indeed, good man, they will treat you according to your heedlessness. That evil deed was not done by your mother, not by your father, not by your brother, not by your sister, not by your friends and companions, not by your relatives, not by devas, not by samaṇas and brahmins. That evil deed was done by you yourself, and you will experience its result.'

title: Possessing these five qualities, bhikkhus, an elder bhikkhu is not loved, not liked, not respected, and not an example for his fellow celibates. What five?…
content: Possessing these five qualities, bhikkhus, an elder bhikkhu is not loved, not liked, not respected, and not an example for his fellow celibates. What five? He is greedy for what is agreeable, hateful toward what is hateful, deluded about what is deluding, angry at what is angering, and infatuated with what is infatuating. Possessing these five qualities, bhikkhus, an elder bhikkhu is not loved, not liked, not respected, and not an example for his fellow celibates.

title: Furthermore, householders, a noble disciple reflects: 'If someone were to commit sexual misconduct with my wife, that would be undesirable and unpleasing for me. But…
content: Furthermore, householders, a noble disciple reflects: 'If someone were to commit sexual misconduct with my wife, that would be undesirable and unpleasing for me. But if I were to commit sexual misconduct with another's wife, that would be undesirable and unpleasing for that person. This state that is undesirable and unpleasing for me is also undesirable and unpleasing for others. Since this state is undesirable and unpleasing for me, why should I impose it on others?' Reflecting thus, he himself abstains from sexual misconduct, encourages others to abstain from sexual misconduct, and speaks in praise of abstaining from sexual misconduct. Thus, in bodily conduct, he becomes completely pure.

title: Bhikkhus, I declare that the stilling of formations is gradual. When one attains the first jhāna, speech is stilled ... For a bhikkhu with taints…
content: Bhikkhus, I declare that the stilling of formations is gradual. When one attains the first jhāna, speech is stilled ... For a bhikkhu with taints destroyed, greed is stilled, hatred is stilled, delusion is stilled. Bhikkhus, there are six kinds of tranquility. When one attains the first jhāna, speech is tranquil. When one attains the second jhāna, initial and sustained thought are tranquil. When one attains the third jhāna, rapture is tranquil. When one attains the fourth jhāna, in-breath and out-breath are tranquil. When one attains the cessation of perception and feeling, perception and feeling are tranquil. For a bhikkhu with taints destroyed, greed is tranquil, hatred is tranquil, delusion is tranquil.

title: Pukkusa, that man then thought: 'How wonderful, how marvelous is the serenity of the renunciant! Though the rain pours down heavily, lightning flashes, thunder crashes,…
content: Pukkusa, that man then thought: 'How wonderful, how marvelous is the serenity of the renunciant! Though the rain pours down heavily, lightning flashes, thunder crashes, and two farmer brothers and four oxen are struck dead by lightning, he sees nothing, hears nothing.' After expressing his faith in me, that man paid homage to me, circumambulated me, and departed.

title: Bhikkhus, for the Tathāgata, the Arahant, the Perfectly Enlightened One, two kinds of thoughts are frequently practiced: the thought of security and the thought of…
content:
Bhikkhus, for the Tathāgata, the Arahant, the Perfectly Enlightened One, two kinds of thoughts are frequently practiced: the thought of security and the thought of seclusion. Bhikkhus, the Tathāgata delights in non-harming, takes pleasure in non-harming. For the Tathāgata, bhikkhus, this delight in non-harming, this pleasure in non-harming, these thoughts are frequently practiced: 'By this conduct I harm no one, whether frail or strong.'

Bhikkhus, the Tathāgata delights in seclusion, takes pleasure in seclusion. For the Tathāgata, bhikkhus, this delight in seclusion, this pleasure in seclusion, these thoughts are frequently practiced: 'Whatever is unwholesome, that is abandoned.'

title: 'Bhikkhus, who in this region is praised by his fellow celibates in the same region thus: 'He himself has few desires and speaks to the…
content: 'Bhikkhus, who in this region is praised by his fellow celibates in the same region thus: 'He himself has few desires and speaks to the bhikkhus about fewness of desires; he himself is content and speaks about contentment; he himself is secluded and speaks about seclusion; he himself is aloof from society and speaks about aloofness; he himself is energetic and diligent and speaks about energy and diligence; he himself possesses virtue and speaks about possession of virtue; he himself possesses concentration and speaks about possession of concentration; he himself possesses wisdom and speaks about possession of wisdom; he himself possesses liberation and speaks about possession of liberation; he himself possesses knowledge and vision of liberation and speaks about possession of knowledge and vision of liberation; he is an instructor, a revealer, a presenter, an exhorter, an inspirer, a gladdening of his fellow celibates'?" "Venerable Sir, the Venerable Puṇṇa, son of Mantāṇī, is the bhikkhu in this region praised by his fellow celibates... gladdening of his fellow celibates." At that time, the Venerable Sāriputta was sitting not far from the Blessed One. The Venerable Sāriputta thought: "Fortunate is the Venerable Puṇṇa, son of Mantāṇī! Truly fortunate is the Venerable Puṇṇa, son of Mantāṇī! He has been praised point by point by wise fellow celibates in the presence of the Teacher, and has been accepted by the Teacher. Perhaps we shall meet the Venerable Puṇṇa, son of Mantāṇī, at some time, in some place, and perhaps have a conversation with him.

title: Friends, a forest-dwelling bhikkhu should be energetic and diligent. Friends, if a forest-dwelling bhikkhu is lazy, then someone might say of him: 'What good is…
content: Friends, a forest-dwelling bhikkhu should be energetic and diligent. Friends, if a forest-dwelling bhikkhu is lazy, then someone might say of him: 'What good is it that this venerable one, a forest-dweller, living alone in the forest, doing as he pleases, now is lazy?' Someone might say this of him. Therefore, a forest-dwelling bhikkhu should be energetic and diligent. Friends, a forest-dwelling bhikkhu should be mindful and clearly comprehending. Friends, if a forest-dwelling bhikkhu is unmindful, then someone might say of him: 'What good is it that this venerable one, a forest-dweller, living alone in the forest, doing as he pleases, now is unmindful?' Someone might say this of him. Therefore, a forest-dwelling bhikkhu should be mindful and clearly comprehending. Friends, a forest-dwelling bhikkhu should have concentration. Friends, if a forest-dwelling bhikkhu does not have concentration, then someone might say of him: 'What good is it that this venerable one, a forest-dweller, living alone in the forest, doing as he pleases, now does not have concentration?' Someone might say this of him. Therefore, a forest-dwelling bhikkhu should have concentration.

title: Bhikkhus, those who are bound by the bond of sensual desire and bound by the bond of existence are those who return, who come back…
content:
Bhikkhus, those who are bound by the bond of sensual desire and bound by the bond of existence are those who return, who come back to this state.

Those who have cut off the bond of sensual desire, bhikkhus, but are still bound by the bond of existence, are non-returners, not coming back to this state.

Those who have cut off the bond of sensual desire and cut off the bond of existence are Arahants, with taints destroyed.

Those who are overcome by the bond of sensual desire,
By the bond of existence, by both bonds,
Those beings go to the round of rebirth,
They go to birth and death.

Those who have abandoned sensual desire
But have not yet reached the destruction of taints,
Still overcome by the bond of existence,
Are called non-returners.

Those who have cut off all doubt,
Destroyed conceit and renewed existence,
Certainly have gone to the far shore in this world,
For they have attained the destruction of taints.
